title: Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Dhun
info: Mantra: Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare
Scale: Based on tutorial (Sa assumed as base)

lines.indian: Ni Ni Sa Sa Ni Dha Ni Ni
Pa Pa Dha Dha Sa Ni Dha Pa
Ni Ni Sa Sa Ni Dha Ni Ni
Pa Pa Dha Dha Sa Ni Dha Pa
Ni Dha Pa Ni Dha Pa
Pa Dha Ni Sa' Ni Dha Pa

description: This 'Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Dhun' is a meditative and uplifting rendition of the Mahamantra, a central chant in the Vaishnava tradition, particularly popularized globally by ISKCON. Its simple, repetitive melody is designed to facilitate focus and devotion, making it accessible for spiritual practice and communal chanting. The dhun creates a serene and immersive atmosphere, inviting listeners into a state of contemplation and inner peace.
trivia: The 'Hare Krishna Mahamantra' is composed of sixteen words: Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare / Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. It is believed to be a potent means of spiritual liberation in the Kali Yuga.

ISKCON (International Society for Krishna Consciousness), founded by A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada in 1966, played a pivotal role in popularizing this mantra and the bhajan style of chanting across the Western world and beyond.

Many artists, both within and outside ISKCON, have created numerous musical interpretations of the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, ranging from traditional Indian classical styles to contemporary Western genres.

The repetitive nature of a 'dhun' (a melodic phrase or tune) is specifically designed to aid concentration during meditation and chanting, allowing the practitioner to immerse themselves fully in the sound vibration of the mantra.
